get.description: List all address maps owned by the account.

post.description: Create a new address map under the account.

description.description: An optional description field which may be used to describe the types of IPs or zones on the map.

enabled.description: Whether the Address Map is enabled or not. Cloudflare's DNS will not respond with IP addresses on an Address Map until the map is enabled.

delete.description: Delete a particular address map owned by the account. An Address Map must be disabled before it can be deleted.

get.description: Show a particular address map owned by the account.

patch.description: Modify properties of an address map owned by the account.

default_sni.description: If you have legacy TLS clients which do not send the TLS server name indicator, then you can specify one default SNI on the map. If Cloudflare receives a TLS handshake from a client without an SNI, it will respond with the default SNI on those IPs. The default SNI can be any valid zone or subdomain owned by the account.

description.description: An optional description field which may be used to describe the types of IPs or zones on the map.

enabled.description: Whether the Address Map is enabled or not. Cloudflare's DNS will not respond with IP addresses on an Address Map until the map is enabled.

delete.description: Remove an account as a member of a particular address map.

200.description: Remove an account membership from an Address Map response

4XX.description: Remove an account membership from an Address Map response failure

delete.summary: Remove an account membership from an Address Map

put.description: Add an account as a member of a particular address map.

200.description: Add an account membership to an Address Map response

4XX.description: Add an account membership to an Address Map response failure

put.summary: Add an account membership to an Address Map

delete.description: Remove an IP from a particular address map.

200.description: Remove an IP from an Address Map response

4XX.description: Remove an IP from an Address Map response failure

delete.summary: Remove an IP from an Address Map

put.description: Add an IP from a prefix owned by the account to a particular address map.

200.description: Add an IP to an Address Map response

4XX.description: Add an IP to an Address Map response failure

put.summary: Add an IP to an Address Map

delete.description: Remove a zone as a member of a particular address map.

200.description: Remove a zone membership from an Address Map response

4XX.description: Remove a zone membership from an Address Map response failure

delete.summary: Remove a zone membership from an Address Map

put.description: Add a zone as a member of a particular address map.

200.description: Add a zone membership to an Address Map response

4XX.description: Add a zone membership to an Address Map response failure

put.summary: Add a zone membership to an Address Map

get.description: List all leases owned by the account.

post.description: Submit LOA document (pdf format) under the account.

get.description: Download specified LOA document under the account.

schema.description: Identifier for the uploaded LOA document.

get.description: List all prefixes owned by the account.

IP Prefixes: BGP On Demand Read

post.description: Add a new prefix under the account.

asn.description: Autonomous System Number (ASN) the prefix will be advertised under.

cidr.description: IP Prefix in Classless Inter-Domain Routing format.

delegate_loa_creation.description: Whether Cloudflare is allowed to generate the LOA document on behalf of the prefix owner.

loa_document_id.description: Identifier for the uploaded LOA document.

delete.description: Delete an unapproved prefix owned by the account.

get.description: List a particular prefix owned by the account.
x-api-token-group: IP Prefixes: BGP On Demand Write
IP Prefixes: BGP On Demand Read

patch.description: Modify the description for a prefix owned by the account.

get.description: List all BGP Prefixes within the specified IP Prefix. BGP Prefixes are used to control which specific subnets are advertised to the Internet. It is possible to advertise subnets more specific than an IP Prefix by creating more specific BGP Prefixes.
x-api-token-group: IP Prefixes: BGP On Demand Write
IP Prefixes: BGP On Demand Read

post.description: Create a BGP prefix, controlling the BGP advertisement status of a specific subnet. When created, BGP prefixes are initially withdrawn, and can be advertised with the Update BGP Prefix API.

delete.description: Delete a BGP Prefix associated with the specified IP Prefix. A BGP Prefix must be withdrawn before it can be deleted.

get.description: Retrieve a single BGP Prefix according to its identifier
x-api-token-group: IP Prefixes: BGP On Demand Write
IP Prefixes: BGP On Demand Read

patch.description: Update the properties of a BGP Prefix, such as the on demand advertisement status (advertised or withdrawn).
x-api-token-group: IP Prefixes: BGP On Demand Write

get.description:
View the current advertisement state for a prefix.

**Deprecated:** Prefer the BGP Prefixes endpoints, which additionally allow for advertising and withdrawing 
subnets of an IP prefix.
x-api-token-group: IP Prefixes: BGP On Demand Write
IP Prefixes: BGP On Demand Read

patch.description:
Advertise or withdraw the BGP route for a prefix.

**Deprecated:** Prefer the BGP Prefixes endpoints, which additionally allow for advertising and withdrawing 
subnets of an IP prefix.

advertised.description:
Advertisement status of the prefix. If `true`, the BGP route for the prefix is advertised to the Internet. If 
`false`, the BGP route is withdrawn.

200.description: Update Prefix Dynamic Advertisement Status response

4XX.description: Update Prefix Dynamic Advertisement Status response failure

x-api-token-group: IP Prefixes: BGP On Demand Write

get.description:
List the Cloudflare services this prefix is currently bound to. Traffic sent to an address within an IP prefix will be routed to the Cloudflare service of the most-specific Service Binding matching the address.
**Example:** binding `192.0.2.0/24` to Cloudflare Magic Transit and `192.0.2.1/32` to the Cloudflare CDN would route traffic for `192.0.2.1` to the CDN, and traffic for all other IPs in the prefix to Cloudflare Magic Transit.

200.description: Service Bindings attached to the Prefix

post.description:
Creates a new Service Binding, routing traffic to IPs within the given CIDR to a service running on Cloudflare's network.
**NOTE:** The first Service Binding created for an IP Prefix must exactly match the IP Prefix's CIDR. Subsequent Service Bindings may be created with a more-specific CIDR. Refer to the  [Service Bindings Documentation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/byoip/service-bindings/) for compatibility details.

200.description: The Service Binding with the requested ID

get.description: List all delegations for a given account IP prefix.

post.description: Create a new account delegation for a given IP prefix.

cidr.description: IP Prefix in Classless Inter-Domain Routing format.

delegated_account_id.description: Account identifier for the account to which prefix is being delegated.

delete.description: Delete an account delegation for a given IP prefix.

post.description: Triggers a new prefix validation. The checks are run asynchronously and include IRR, RPKI, and prefix ownership.

get.description: List all Regional Services regions available for use by this account.

label.description: Human-readable text label for the region

get.description: Bring-Your-Own IP (BYOIP) prefixes onboarded to Cloudflare must be bound to a service running on the Cloudflare network to enable a Cloudflare product on the IP addresses. This endpoint can be used as a reference of available services on the Cloudflare network, and their service IDs.

id.description:
Identifier of a Service on the Cloudflare network. Available services and their IDs may be found in the
**List Services** endpoint.

name.description: Name of a service running on the Cloudflare network

get.description: List all Regional Hostnames within a zone.

post.description: Create a new Regional Hostname entry. Cloudflare will only use data centers that are physically located within the chosen region to decrypt and service HTTPS traffic. Learn more about [Regional Services](https://developers.cloudflare.com/data-localization/regional-services/get-started/).

hostname.description: DNS hostname to be regionalized, must be a subdomain of the zone. Wildcards are supported for one level, e.g `*.example.com`

routing.description: Configure which routing method to use for the regional hostname

delete.description: Delete the region configuration for a specific Regional Hostname.

get.description: Fetch the configuration for a specific Regional Hostname, within a zone.

patch.description: Update the configuration for a specific Regional Hostname. Only the region_key of a hostname is mutable.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

Advertisement status of the prefix. If `true`, the BGP route for the prefix is advertised to the Internet. If 
`false`, the BGP route is withdrawn.

advertised_modified_at.description: Last time the advertisement status was changed. This field is only not 'null' if on demand is enabled.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

cidr.description: IP Prefix in Classless Inter-Domain Routing format.

service_id.description:

addressing_ips.description: The set of IPs on the Address Map.

asn_prepend_count.description: Number of times to prepend the Cloudflare ASN to the BGP AS-Path attribute

auto_advertise_withdraw.description: Determines if Cloudflare advertises a BYOIP BGP prefix even when there is no matching BGP prefix in the Magic routing table. When true, Cloudflare will automatically withdraw the BGP prefix when there are no matching BGP routes, and will resume advertising when there is at least one matching BGP route.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

identifier.description: The identifier for the membership (eg. a zone or account tag).

advertised.description: Prefix advertisement status to the Internet. This field is only not 'null' if on demand is enabled.

advertised_modified_at.description: Last time the advertisement status was changed. This field is only not 'null' if on demand is enabled.

approved.description: Approval state of the prefix (P = pending, V = active).

cidr.description: IP Prefix in Classless Inter-Domain Routing format.

delegated_account_id.description: Account identifier for the account to which prefix is being delegated.

can_delete.description: If set to false, then the Address Map cannot be deleted via API. This is true for Cloudflare-managed maps.

can_modify_ips.description: If set to false, then the IPs on the Address Map cannot be modified via the API. This is true for Cloudflare-managed maps.

default_sni.description: If you have legacy TLS clients which do not send the TLS server name indicator, then you can specify one default SNI on the map. If Cloudflare receives a TLS handshake from a client without an SNI, it will respond with the default SNI on those IPs. The default SNI can be any valid zone or subdomain owned by the account.

description.description: An optional description field which may be used to describe the types of IPs or zones on the map.

enabled.description: Whether the Address Map is enabled or not. Cloudflare's DNS will not respond with IP addresses on an Address Map until the map is enabled.

identifier.description: The identifier for the membership (eg. a zone or account tag).

auto_advertise_withdraw.description: Determines if Cloudflare advertises a BYOIP BGP prefix even when there is no matching BGP prefix in the Magic routing table. When true, Cloudflare will automatically withdraw the BGP prefix when there are no matching BGP routes, and will resume advertising when there is at least one matching BGP route.
